_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 8-6 Dispatch (Revised 3/24/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan System Protocol Section 8-6: Dispatch — MDHHS Approved 3/24/2023
purpose: Govern EMS dispatch processes, pre-arrival instructions, and dispatch-level prearrival protocols. Dispatch plays a critical role in initiating appropriate response, gathering essential patient information, and providing pre-arrival instructions that may save lives before EMS arrival.

sections.title: Dispatch Response Priority
points: Dispatch must assign appropriate response priority based on caller information using a medically approved dispatch protocol (e.g., Medical Priority Dispatch System — MPDS or equivalent)
Priority 1 (Emergency): L&S response — life-threatening conditions
Priority 2 (Urgent): Expedited response — urgent but not immediately life-threatening
Priority 3 (Non-Emergency): Routine response
Dispatch protocols must include criteria for upgrading or downgrading response priority based on additional caller information
MCAs must have a medically approved dispatch protocol in use within their jurisdiction

sections.title: Pre-Arrival Instructions
points: Dispatch must provide pre-arrival instructions for life-threatening conditions including:
— CPR instructions for cardiac arrest (call before confirm protocol if indicated)
— Airway positioning instructions
— Bleeding control instructions
— Instructions for imminent delivery
— Choking instructions
Pre-arrival instructions must follow MCA-approved dispatch scripts
Dispatch must remain on the line to support callers providing care until EMS arrives when feasible

sections.title: Information Gathering
points: Dispatch must obtain: location, callback number, chief complaint, patient age and gender, level of consciousness, and any known hazards
For cardiac arrest calls: confirm pulselessness and breathing status; initiate CPR instructions immediately
Scene safety information: weapons present, hazmat, violence, access difficulties — relay to responding units
Notify responding units of any additional information received after initial dispatch

sections.title: Multi-Agency Coordination
points: Dispatch must notify responding units of co-responding agencies (fire, law enforcement, air medical)
Dispatch must coordinate ALS intercept requests per MCA protocol (Section 8-5)
Major incidents: activate MCI protocol as directed by MCA; notify appropriate supervisory and administrative contacts
Air medical activation: follow criteria per Section 8-9 Helicopter Utilization Protocol

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must have a medically approved dispatch protocol adopted for their jurisdiction
options: MCA-approved equivalent medically-directed dispatch protocol adopted

deterioration_flags: Caller reports patient deterioration after initial dispatch — upgrade response immediately; initiate appropriate pre-arrival instructions
Cardiac arrest reported — initiate CPR instructions immediately; do not wait for unit confirmation
scope_notes: All EMS levels — dispatch is a system function
MCA must have medically approved dispatch protocol
Pre-arrival instructions mandatory for: cardiac arrest, airway, bleeding, delivery, choking
Dispatch must relay all additional scene/patient information to responding units
Major incidents: activate MCI protocol per MCA
